revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hijirizuka Kofun - Ayamezuka Kofun]][[ja:聖塚・菖蒲塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Hijirizuka Kofun - Ayamezuka Kofun | native_name =聖塚・菖蒲塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hijirizuka Kofun and Ayamezuka Kofun (Ayabe).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hijirizuka Kofun (right) and Ayamezuka Kofun (left rear) | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hijirizuka Kofun - Ayamezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|19|17.82|N|135|16|17.67|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ayabe|qid=Q694370|1=Ayabe, Kyoto}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.mid-5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|19|17.82|N|135|16|17.67|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Hijirizuka Kofun - Ayamezuka Kofun'''|聖塚・菖蒲塚古墳|}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Tada neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ayabe, Kyoto|en|Ayabe, Kyoto|qid=Q694370|lt=Ayabe, Kyoto|1=Ayabe, Kyoto}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138774 |title=聖塚・菖蒲塚古墳|trans-title= Hijirizuka Kofun - Ayamezuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Hijirizuka Kofun and Ayamezuka Kofun are both rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|hōfun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumuli. They are located about 120 meters apart on the Hatta River, a tributary of the {{ill|Yura River (Japan)|en|Yura River (Japan)|qid=Q11577617|lt=Yura River|1=Yura River (Japan)}}, in the central northern part of Kyoto Prefecture, about 2.5 kilometers from the center of the city of Ayase. Both are estimated to have been built around the first half of the 5th century in the middleof the Kofun period, and are among the largest in the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba region|1=Tanba Province}}. The Hijirizuka Kofun was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1894, at which time a large quantity {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were found. These included a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, iron swords, halberd, armor, ''[[magatama]]'', and more than 30 glass balls. The mound was found to have been constructed in two-tiers, with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. The mound measured 54 meters on each side, with a height of seven meters and was surrounded by a moat with a width ranging from 10.3 to 13 meters. Another excavation was made on both tumuli in 1983, during which time was discovered that the Hijirizuka Kofun had a structure on the center of its southern side, which measured 17.5 meters east-west by 4.6 meters north-south. Many haniwa were found around this location, indicating that it had some ceremonial or ritual significance.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Ayamezuka Kofun measures 32 meters on each side, and has a height of 5.1 meters. It also had ''fukiishi'' and ''haniwa'' rows, and likewise was found to have a structure in the center of its southern side. However, in the case of the Ayamezuka Kofun, it was trapezoidal, with measurements of 4.5 meters east-west and 3.2 meters north-south. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is about a seven-minute walk from {{ill|Ayabe Station|en|Ayabe Station|qid=Q871639|lt=Ayabe Station|1=Ayabe Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Maizuru Line|en|Maizuru Line|qid=Q898812|lt=Maizuru Line|1=Maizuru Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Hijirizuka Kofun (Ayabe), funkyu.jpg|Hijirizuka Kofun Ayamezuka Kofun (Ayabe), funkyu.jpg|Ayamezuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|qid=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
